pulse: The consumer sector currently trades at median EV/EBITDA multiple of 15.5x and a median P/E of 27.8x, in line with broader market averages. Despite the optimistic revenue growth forecast of 4.6%, December retail sales being flat fell well short of expectations, notably impacting companies like Walmart (WMT), which has an elevated EV/EBITDA of 24.4x and a trailing P/E of 46.6x, reflecting investor concerns amid rising consumer debt and a tightening economic environment. Looking ahead, subdued consumer spending could pressure margins and growth for many players, prompting investors to exercise caution as they navigate potential earnings revisions stemming from recent disappointing sales reports.
